english.text: Ibn Idris and Ibn ‘Abbad narrated to us from Shu‘bah, from ‘Adi ibn Thabit, from Sa‘id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn ‘Abbas, who said: “The Messenger of Allah ﷺ went out on Eid day and led the people in prayer, and he did not pray before it or after it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ أَبَانَ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْبَجَلِيِّ، عَنْ أَبِي بَكْرِ بْنِ حَفْصٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ أَنَّهُ خَرَجَ يَوْمَ عِيدٍ، فَلَمْ يُصَلِّ قَبْلَهَا وَلَا بَعْدَهَا، وَذَكَرَ أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فَعَلَهُ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Aban ibn ‘Abd Allah al-Bajali → Abu Bakr ibn Hafs → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Aban ibn ‘Abd Allah al-Bajali, from Abu Bakr ibn Hafs, from Ibn ‘Umar, that he went out on Eid day and did not pray before it or after it, and he mentioned that the Prophet ﷺ did that.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، وَابْنُ عَبَّادَ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ ابْنَ أَبِي أَوْفَى، وَابْنَ عَمْرٍو، وَجَابِرَ بْنَ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، وَشُرَيْحًا، وَابْنَ مَعْقِلٍ لَا يُصَلُّونَ قَبْلَ الْعِيدِ وَلَا بَعْدَهُ

english.narrator: Ibn Idris/Ibn ‘Abbad → Layth → Al-Sha‘bi
english.text: Ibn Idris and Ibn ‘Abbad narrated to us from Layth, from Al-Sha‘bi, who said: “I saw Ibn Abi Awfa, Ibn ‘Amr, Jabir ibn ‘Abd Allah, Shurayh, and Ibn Ma‘qil not praying before Eid nor after it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ عَنْ أَبِي بِشْرٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، قَالَ: كُنْتُ مَعَهُ جَالِسًا فِي الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ يَوْمَ الْفِطْرِ، فَقَامَ عَطَاءٌ يُصَلِّي قَبْلَ خُرُوجِ الْإِمَامِ، فَأَوْمَأَ إِلَيْهِ سَعِيدٌ أَنْ اجْلِسْ، فَجَلَسَ عَطَاءٌ، قَالَ: فَقُلْتُ لِسَعِيدٍ: عَمَّنْ هَذَا يَا أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ؟ فَقَالَ: عَنْ حُذَيْفَةَ وَأَصْحَابِهِ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Abu Bishr → Sa‘id ibn Jubayr
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us from Abu Bishr, from Sa‘id ibn Jubayr, who said: I was sitting with him in the Sacred Mosque on the day of Fitr. ‘Ata’ stood up to pray before the Imam came out. Sa‘id gestured to him to sit down, so ‘Ata’ sat down. I said to Sa‘id: “From whom is this [ruling], O Abu ‘Abd Allah?” He said: “From Hudhayfah and his companions.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَرْوَانُ بْنُ مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ سُمَيْعٍ، عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي كَثِيرٍ، أَنَّ أَبَا مَسْعُودٍ الْأَنْصَارِيَّ، كَانَ إِذَا كَانَ يَوْمَ أَضْحَى أَوْ يَوْمَ فِطْرٍ طَافَ فِي الصُّفُوفِ، فَقَالَ: لَا صَلَاةَ إِلَّا مَعَ الْإِمَامِ

english.narrator: Marwan ibn Mu‘awiyah → Isma‘il ibn Sumay‘ → ‘Ali ibn Abi Kathir → Abu Mas‘ud al-Ansari
english.text: Marwan ibn Mu‘awiyah narrated to us from Isma‘il ibn Sumay‘, from ‘Ali ibn Abi Kathir, that when it was the day of Adha or Fitr, Abu Mas‘ud al-Ansari would go around the rows saying: “There is no prayer except with the Imam.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ بْنِ أَبِي الشَّعْثَاءِ، عَنِ الْأَسْوَدِ بْنِ هِلَالٍ، عَنْ ثَعْلَبَةَ بْنِ زَهْدَمٍ الْحَنْظَلِيِّ، أَنَّ أَبَا مَسْعُودٍ الْأَنْصَارِيَّ، قَامَ فِي يَوْمِ عِيدٍ، فَقَالَ: إِنَّهُ لَا صَلَاةَ فِي هَذَا الْيَوْمِ حَتَّى يَخْرُجَ الْإِمَامُ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Sufyan → Ash‘ath ibn Abi al-Sha‘tha’ → Al-Aswad ibn Hilal → Tha‘labah ibn Zahdam al-Hanzali → Abu Mas‘ud al-Ansari
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Sufyan, from Ash‘ath ibn Abi al-Sha‘tha’, from Al-Aswad ibn Hilal, from Tha‘labah ibn Zahdam al-Hanzali, that Abu Mas‘ud al-Ansari stood up on Eid day and said: “There is no prayer on this day until the Imam comes out.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ أَنَّهُ كَانَ لَا يُصَلِّي قَبْلَ الْعِيدِ، وَلَا بَعْدَهُ

english.narrator: Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Ayyub → Nafi‘ → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us from Ayyub, from Nafi‘, from Ibn ‘Umar, that he used to not pray before Eid nor after it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحِيمِ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ مُجَالِدٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: كُنْتُ بَيْنَ مَسْرُوقٍ وَشُرَيْحٍ فِي يَوْمِ عِيدٍ، فَلَمْ يُصَلِّيَا قَبْلَهَا وَلَا بَعْدَهَا

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-Rahim ibn Sulayman → Mujalid → Al-Sha‘bi
english.text: ‘Abd al-Rahim ibn Sulayman narrated to us from Mujalid, from Al-Sha‘bi, who said: “I was between Masruq and Shurayh on an Eid day, and neither of them prayed before it nor after it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ سِيرِينَ، قَالَ: كَانَ لَا يُصَلِّي قَبْلَ الْعِيدِ، وَلَا بَعْدَهُ

english.narrator: Ibn Idris → Hisham → Ibn Sirin
english.text: Ibn Idris narrated to us from Hisham, from Ibn Sirin, who said: “He used to not pray before Eid nor after it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، قَالَ: رَأَى الشَّعْبِيُّ، إِنْسَانًا يُصَلِّي بَعْدَمَا انْصَرَفَ الْإِمَامُ، فَجَبَذَهُ

english.narrator: Ibn Idris → Isma‘il → Al-Sha‘bi
english.text: Ibn Idris narrated to us from Isma‘il, who said: “Al-Sha‘bi saw a person praying after the Imam had finished, so he pulled him.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سَلَمَةَ، عَنِ الضَّحَّاكِ، قَالَ: لَا صَلَاةَ قَبْلَهَا وَلَا بَعْدَهَا

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Salamah → Al-Dahhak
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Salamah, from Al-Dahhak, who said: “There is no prayer before it or after it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ أَبِي خَالِدٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ أَنَّهُ كَانَ لَا يُصَلِّي قَبْلَهَا وَلَا بَعْدَهَا

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Isma‘il ibn Abi Khalid → Al-Sha‘bi
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Isma‘il ibn Abi Khalid, from Al-Sha‘bi, that he used to not pray before it or after it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عُبَيْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مُوسَى، قَالَ: أنا إِسْرَائِيلُ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْأَصَمِّ، أَنَّهُ خَرَجَ مَعَ مَسْرُوقٍ، فِي يَوْمِ عِيدٍ، فَقُمْتُ أُصَلِّي فَأَخَذَ بِثِيَابِي فَأَجْلَسَنِي، ثُمَّ قَالَ: لَا صَلَاةَ حَتَّى يُصَلِّيَ الْإِمَامُ

english.narrator: ‘Ubayd Allah ibn Musa → Isra’il → Abu Ishaq → ‘Amr ibn ‘Abd Allah al-Asamm
english.text: ‘Ubayd Allah ibn Musa narrated to us, saying: Isra’il informed us from Abu Ishaq, from ‘Amr ibn ‘Abd Allah al-Asamm, that he went out with Masruq on an Eid day. I stood up to pray, so he grabbed my clothes and made me sit down, then said: “There is no prayer until the Imam prays.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: أنا أَبُو الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، قَالَ: كَانَ سَعِيدُ بْنُ جُبَيْرٍ، وَإِبْرَاهِيمُ، وَعَلْقَمَةُ يُصَلُّونَ بَعْدَ الْعِيدِ أَرْبَعًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu al-Ahwas → Abu Ishaq
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu al-Ahwas informed us from Abu Ishaq, who said: “Sa‘id ibn Jubayr, Ibrahim, and ‘Alqamah used to pray four [rak‘ahs] after Eid.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبِي زِيَادٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، وَسَعِيدَ بْنَ جُبَيْرٍ، وَمُجَاهِدًا، وَعَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى يُصَلُّونَ بَعْدَهَا أَرْبَعًا

english.narrator: ‘Abd Allah ibn Idris → Yazid ibn Abi Ziyad
english.text: ‘Abd Allah ibn Idris narrated to us from Yazid ibn Abi Ziyad, who said: I saw Ibrahim, Sa‘id ibn Jubayr, and Mujahid—and from ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Layla—praying four [rak‘ahs] after it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: كَانَ عَلْقَمَةُ، يَجِيءُ يَوْمَ الْعِيدِ، فَيَجْلِسُ فِي الْمُصَلَّى وَلَا يُصَلِّي حَتَّى يُصَلِّيَ الْإِمَامُ، فَإِذَا صَلَّى الْإِمَامُ، قَامَ فَصَلَّى أَرْبَعًا

english.narrator: Jarir → Mansur → Ibrahim
english.text: Jarir narrated to us from Mansur, from Ibrahim, who said: “‘Alqamah used to come on Eid day and sit in the prayer place, not praying until the Imam prayed. When the Imam prayed, he would stand up and pray four [rak‘ahs].”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَرْوَانُ بْنُ مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنْ صَالِحِ بْنِ حَيٍّ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ: كَانَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ، إِذَا رَجَعَ يَوْمَ الْعِيدِ صَلَّى فِي أَهْلِهِ أَرْبَعًا

english.narrator: Marwan ibn Mu‘awiyah → Salih ibn Hayy → Al-Sha‘bi
english.text: Marwan ibn Mu‘awiyah narrated to us from Salih ibn Hayy, from Al-Sha‘bi, who said: I heard him say: “‘Abd Allah used to pray four [rak‘ahs] with his family when he returned on Eid day.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ مِسْعَرٍ، عَنْ أَبِي صَخْرَةَ، عَنِ الْأَسْوَدِ بْنِ هِلَالٍ، قَالَ: خَرَجْتُ مَعَ عَلِيٍّ، فَلَمَّا صَلَّى الْإِمَامُ، قَامَ فَصَلَّى بَعْدَهَا أَرْبَعًا

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Mis‘ar → Abu Sakhrah → Al-Aswad ibn Hilal
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Mis‘ar, from Abu Sakhrah, from Al-Aswad ibn Hilal, who said: I went out with ‘Ali. When the Imam prayed, he stood up and prayed four [rak‘ahs] after it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصُ بْنُ غِيَاثٍ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنْ عَلْقَمَةَ، وَأَصْحَابِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ أَنَّهُمْ كَانُوا يُصَلُّونَ بَعْدَ الْعِيدِ أَرْبَعًا

english.narrator: Hafs ibn Ghiyath → Al-A‘mash → Ibrahim → ‘Alqamah
english.text: Hafs ibn Ghiyath narrated to us from Al-A‘mash, from Ibrahim, from ‘Alqamah and the companions of ‘Abd Allah, that they used to pray four [rak‘ahs] after Eid.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: كَانُوا يُصَلُّونَ بَعْدَ الْعِيدِ أَرْبَعًا، وَلَا يُصَلُّونَ قَبْلَهَا شَيْئًا

english.narrator: Abu Mu‘awiyah → Al-A‘mash → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Mu‘awiyah narrated to us from Al-A‘mash, from Ibrahim, who said: “They used to pray four [rak‘ahs] after Eid, but they would not pray anything before it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ، عَنْ عَاصِمٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ الْحَسَنَ، وَابْنَ سِيرِينَ يُصَلِّيَانِ بَعْدَ الْعِيدِ وَيُطِيلَانِ الْقِيَامَ

english.narrator: ‘Abdah → ‘Asim
english.text: ‘Abdah narrated to us from ‘Asim, who said: “I saw Al-Hasan and Ibn Sirin praying after Eid and prolonging the standing.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا شَبَابَةُ بْنُ سَوَّارٍ، قَالَ: نَا الْمُغِيرَةُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ بُرَيْدَةَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يُصَلِّي يَوْمَ الْعِيدِ قَبْلَ الصَّلَاةِ أَرْبَعًا، وَبَعْدَهَا أَرْبَعًا

english.narrator: Shababah ibn Sawwar → Al-Mughirah ibn Muslim → ‘Abd Allah ibn Buraydah → His Father
english.text: Shababah ibn Sawwar narrated to us, saying: Al-Mughirah ibn Muslim narrated to us from ‘Abd Allah ibn Buraydah, from his father, that he used to pray four [rak‘ahs] before the prayer on Eid day, and four after it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ بْنُ حُمَيْدٍ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: كَانَ الْأَسْوَدُ يُصَلِّي قَبْلَ الْعِيدِ قَالَ: وَكَانَ عَلْقَمَةُ لَا يُصَلِّي قَبْلَهَا، وَيُصَلِّي بَعْدَهَا أَرْبَعًا

english.narrator: ‘Abd ibn Humayd → Mansur → Ibrahim
english.text: ‘Abd ibn Humayd narrated to us from Mansur, from Ibrahim, who said: “Al-Aswad used to pray before Eid.” He said: “And ‘Alqamah used not to pray before it, but he would pray four after it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: كَفَاكَ بِقَوْلِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، يَعْنِي: فِي الصَّلَاةِ بَعْدَ الْعِيدِ

english.narrator: Ibn Idris → Shu‘bah → Al-Hakam → Ibrahim
english.text: Ibn Idris narrated to us from Shu‘bah, from Al-Hakam, from Ibrahim, who said: “The saying of ‘Abd Allah suffices you.” Meaning: regarding prayer after Eid.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ أَنَسَ بْنَ مَالِكٍ، وَالْحَسَنَ يُصَلِّيَانِ قَبْلَ خُرُوجِ الْإِمَامِ يَعْنِي يَوْمَ الْعِيدِ

english.narrator: Isma‘il ibn ‘Ulayyah → Ayyub
english.text: Isma‘il ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us from Ayyub, who said: “I saw Anas ibn Malik and Al-Hasan praying before the Imam came out.” Meaning on Eid day.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عَرُوبَةَ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، أَنَّ أَبَا بَرْزَةَ كَانَ يُصَلِّي فِي الْعِيدِ قَبْلَ خُرُوجِ الْإِمَامِ

english.narrator: Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Ibn Abi ‘Arubah → Qatadah
english.text: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us from Ibn Abi ‘Arubah, from Qatadah, that Abu Barzah used to pray on Eid before the Imam came out.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُعَاذُ بْنُ مُعَاذٍ، عَنِ التَّيْمِيِّ، أَنَّهُ رَأَى أَنَسًا، وَالْحَسَنَ، وَسَعِيدَ بْنَ أَبِي الْحَسَنِ، وَجَابِرَ بْنَ زَيْدٍ يُصَلُّونَ قَبْلَ خُرُوجِ الْإِمَامِ فِي الْعِيدَيْنِ

english.narrator: Mu‘adh ibn Mu‘adh → Al-Taymi
english.text: Mu‘adh ibn Mu‘adh narrated to us from Al-Taymi, that he saw Anas, Al-Hasan, Sa‘id ibn Abi al-Hasan, and Jabir ibn Zayd praying before the Imam came out on the two Eids.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُعَاذٌ، عَنِ التَّيْمِيِّ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الدَّانَاجِ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ أَبَا بَرْزَةَ، يَفْعَلُهُ

english.narrator: Mu‘adh → Al-Taymi → ‘Abd Allah al-Danaj
english.text: Mu‘adh narrated to us from Al-Taymi, from ‘Abd Allah al-Danaj, who said: “I saw Abu Barzah doing it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ بُرْدٍ، عَنْ مَكْحُولٍ أَنَّهُ كَانَ يُصَلِّي يَوْمَ الْفِطْرِ وَالنَّحْرِ قَبْلَ خُرُوجِ الْإِمَامِ

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-A‘la → Burd → Makhul
english.text: ‘Abd al-A‘la narrated to us from Burd, from Makhul, that he used to pray on the day of Fitr and Nahr before the Imam came out.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، عَنِ الْأَسْوَدِ أَنَّهُ كَانَ يُصَلِّي يَوْمَ الْعِيدِ قَبْلَ خُرُوجِ الْإِمَامِ

english.narrator: Ghundar → Shu‘bah → Al-Hakam → Al-Aswad
english.text: Ghundar narrated to us from Shu‘bah, from Al-Hakam, from Al-Aswad, that he used to pray on Eid day before the Imam came out.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا سَهْلُ بْنُ يُوسُفَ، عَنِ التَّيْمِيِّ، عَنِ الْأَزْرَقِ بْنِ قَيْسٍ، عَنْ رَجُلٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ رِجَالًا مِنْ أَصْحَابِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، جَاءُوا يَوْمَ عِيدٍ فَصَلَّوْا قَبْلَ خُرُوجِ الْإِمَامِ

english.narrator: Sahl ibn Yusuf → Al-Taymi → Al-Azraq ibn Qays → A man
english.text: Sahl ibn Yusuf narrated to us from Al-Taymi, from Al-Azraq ibn Qays, from a man who said: “I saw men from the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ coming on Eid day and praying before the Imam came out.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنِ الْجَرِيرِيِّ، عَنْ خَالِدٍ الْأَحْدَبِ، عَنْ عَمِّهِ صَفْوَانَ بْنَ مُحْرِزٍ، قَالَ: كَانَتْ صَلَاةُ صَفْوَانَ يَوْمَ الْفِطْرِ وَالنَّحْرِ عَشْرَ رَكَعَاتٍ قَبْلَ خُرُوجِ الْإِمَامِ، وَرَكْعَتَيْنِ بَعْدَ الْإِمَامِ

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-A‘la → Al-Jurayri → Khalid al-Ahdab → Safwan ibn Muhriz
english.text: ‘Abd al-A‘la narrated to us from Al-Jurayri, from Khalid al-Ahdab, from his uncle Safwan ibn Muhriz, who said: “Safwan’s prayer on the day of Fitr and Nahr was ten rak‘ahs before the Imam came out, and two rak‘ahs after the Imam.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو خَالِدٍ الْأَحْمَرُ، عَنِ التَّيْمِيِّ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ أَنَسًا، وَالْحَسَنَ، وَسَعِيدَ بْنَ أَبِي الْحَسَنِ يُصَلُّونَ يَوْمَ الْعِيدِ قَبْلَ خُرُوجِ الْإِمَامِ

english.narrator: Abu Khalid al-Ahmar → Al-Taymi
english.text: Abu Khalid al-Ahmar narrated to us from Al-Taymi, who said: “I saw Anas, Al-Hasan, and Sa‘id ibn Abi al-Hasan praying on Eid day before the Imam came out.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنِ الْحَارِثِ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، قَالَ: كَانَ قَرَأَ فِي الْعِيدَيْنِ أَسْمَعَ مَنْ يَلِيهِ، وَلَا يَجْهَرُ ذَلِكَ الْجَهْرَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu al-Ahwas → Abu Ishaq → Al-Harith → ‘Ali
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu al-Ahwas narrated to us from Abu Ishaq, from Al-Harith, from ‘Ali, who said: “He used to recite in the two Eids loud enough for those next to him to hear, but not very loudly.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عُمَرُ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، قَالَ: يَرْفَعُ الصَّوْتَ بِالْقِرَاءَةِ فِي الْجُمُعَةِ وَالْعِيدَيْنِ

english.narrator: ‘Umar → Ibn Jurayj → ‘Ata’
english.text: ‘Umar narrated to us from Ibn Jurayj, from ‘Ata’, who said: “The voice is raised in recitation during Jumu‘ah and the two Eids.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، وَابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، قَالَ: الْغُسْلُ يَوْمَ الْأَضْحَى وَيَوْمَ الْفِطْرِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Jarir/Ibn Idris → Yazid ibn Abi Layla
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Jarir and Ibn Idris narrated to us from Yazid ibn Abi Layla, who said: “The bathing is on the day of Adha and the day of Fitr.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مُرَّةَ، عَنْ زَاذَانَ، أَنَّ رَجُلًا سَأَلَ عَلِيًّا، عَنِ الْغُسْلِ، فَقَالَ: الْغُسْلُ يَوْمَ الْأَضْحَى وَيَوْمَ الْفِطْرِ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Shu‘bah → ‘Amr ibn Murrah → Zadhan → ‘Ali
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Shu‘bah, from ‘Amr ibn Murrah, from Zadhan, that a man asked ‘Ali about bathing. He said: “The bathing is on the day of Adha and the day of Fitr.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ نُمَيْرٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عُبَيْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عُمَرَ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنَ عُمَرَ أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَغْتَسِلُ لِلْعِيدَيْنِ

english.narrator: ‘Abd Allah ibn Numayr → ‘Ubayd Allah ibn ‘Umar → Nafi‘ → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: ‘Abd Allah ibn Numayr narrated to us, saying: ‘Ubayd Allah ibn ‘Umar narrated to us from Nafi‘, from Ibn ‘Umar, that he used to bathe for the two Eids.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ، عَنْ رَجُلٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: اغْتَسَلَ فِي الْعِيدَيْنِ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Sa‘id ibn Sinan → A man → Ibn ‘Abbas
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Sa‘id ibn Sinan, from a man, from Ibn ‘Abbas, who said: “He bathed on the two Eids.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ الْعُمَرِيِّ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَغْتَسِلُ فِي الْعِيدَيْنِ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Al-‘Umari → Nafi‘ → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Al-‘Umari, from Nafi‘, from Ibn ‘Umar, that he used to bathe on the two Eids.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَغْتَسِلُ يَوْمَ الْفِطْرِ وَيَوْمَ النَّحْرِ

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-A‘la → Yunus → Al-Hasan
english.text: ‘Abd al-A‘la narrated to us from Yunus, from Al-Hasan, that he used to bathe on the day of Fitr and the day of Nahr.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، وَمُحَمَّدٍ أَنَّهُمَا كَانَا يَغْتَسِلَانِ يَوْمَ الْفِطْرِ وَيَوْمَ النَّحْرِ

english.narrator: Abu Usamah → Hisham → Al-Hasan/Muhammad
english.text: Abu Usamah narrated to us from Hisham, from Al-Hasan and Muhammad, that they both used to bathe on the day of Fitr and the day of Nahr.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ حَسَنٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْكَرِيمِ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، قَالَ: كَانُوا يَسْتَحِبُّونَ أَنْ يَغْتَسِلُوا يَوْمَ الْأَضْحَى وَيَوْمَ الْفِطْرِ

english.narrator: Humayd ibn ‘Abd al-Rahman → Hasan → Ibn Abi Layla → ‘Abd al-Karim → Mujahid
english.text: Humayd ibn ‘Abd al-Rahman narrated to us from Hasan, from Ibn Abi Layla, from ‘Abd al-Karim, from Mujahid, who said: “They used to like to bathe on the day of Adha and the day of Fitr.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ، عَنْ زَمْعَةَ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ، أَنَّهُ سَمِعَهُ يَقُولُ: الِاغْتِسَالُ يَوْمَ الْأَضْحَى وَيَوْمَ الْفِطْرِ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَخْرُجَ

english.narrator: Abu Dawud → Zam‘ah → Al-Zuhri → Sa‘id ibn al-Musayyib
english.text: Abu Dawud narrated to us from Zam‘ah, from Al-Zuhri, from Sa‘id ibn al-Musayyib, that he heard him say: “Bathing on the day of Adha and the day of Fitr is before you go out.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَعْنُ بْنُ عِيسَى، عَنْ خَالِدٍ بْنِ أَبِي بَكْرٍ، أَنَّ سَالِمَ بْنَ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ كَانَ يَغْتَسِلُ لِلْعِيدِ

english.narrator: Ma‘n ibn ‘Isa → Khalid ibn Abi Bakr → Salim ibn ‘Abd Allah
english.text: Ma‘n ibn ‘Isa narrated to us from Khalid ibn Abi Bakr, that Salim ibn ‘Abd Allah used to bathe for Eid.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَعْنُ بْنُ عِيسَى، عَنْ خَالِدٍ بْنِ أَبِي بَكْرٍ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ عُبَيْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ يَأْمُرُ بِالْغُسْلِ لِلْعِيدَيْنِ

english.narrator: Ma‘n ibn ‘Isa → Khalid ibn Abi Bakr → ‘Ubayd Allah ibn ‘Abd Allah
english.text: Ma‘n ibn ‘Isa narrated to us from Khalid ibn Abi Bakr, who said: I heard ‘Ubayd Allah ibn ‘Abd Allah ordering bathing for the two Eids.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، وَالْفَضْلُ بْنُ دُكَيْنٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ زِرٍّ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ التَّيْمِيِّ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَسْتَحِبُّ الْغُسْلَ لِلْجُمُعَةِ وَالْعِيدَيْنِ

english.narrator: Waki‘/Al-Fadl ibn Dukayn → Ibn Zirr → Ibrahim al-Taymi → His Father
english.text: Waki‘ and Al-Fadl ibn Dukayn narrated to us from Ibn Zirr, from Ibrahim al-Taymi, from his father, that he used to prefer bathing for Jumu‘ah and the two Eids.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَزْهَرُ بْنُ عَوْنٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدٍ أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَغْتَسِلُ يَوْمَ الْعِيدِ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَغْدُوَ

english.narrator: Azhar ibn ‘Awn → Muhammad
english.text: Azhar ibn ‘Awn narrated to us from Muhammad, that he used to bathe on Eid day before going out.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصُ بْنُ غِيَاثٍ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ عَابِسٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يُخْرِجُ بَنَاتِهِ وَنِسَاءَهُ إِلَى الْعِيدَيْنِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ḥafṣ ibn Ghiyāth → Ḥajjāj → ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn ‘Ābis → Ibn ‘Abbās
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ḥafṣ ibn Ghiyāth narrated to us, from Ḥajjāj, from ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn ‘Ābis, from Ibn ‘Abbās, who said: “The Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to take his daughters and wives out to the two Eids.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنْ طَلْحَةَ الْيَامِيِّ، قَالَ: قَالَ أَبُو بَكْرٍ: حَقٌّ عَلَى كُلِّ ذَاتِ نِطَاقٍ الْخُرُوجُ إِلَى الْعِيدَيْنِ

english.narrator: Ḥafṣ → Al-Ḥasan → ‘Ubayd Allāh → Ṭalḥah al-Yāmī
english.text: Ḥafṣ narrated to us, from Al-Ḥasan, from ‘Ubayd Allāh, from Ṭalḥah al-Yāmī, who said: Abū Bakr said: “It is a duty upon every woman who wears a waistband (i.e., has reached maturity) to go out to the two Eids.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنِ الْحَارِثِ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، قَالَ: حَقٌّ عَلَى كُلِّ ذَاتِ نِطَاقٍ أَنْ تَخْرُجَ إِلَى الْعِيدَيْنِ، وَلَمْ يَكُنْ يُرَخِّصُ لَهُنَّ فِي شَيْءٍ مِنَ الْخُرُوجِ إِلَّا إِلَى الْعِيدَيْنِ

english.narrator: Abū al-Aḥwaṣ → Abū Isḥāq → Al-Ḥārith → ‘Alī
english.text: Abū al-Aḥwaṣ narrated to us, from Abū Isḥāq, from Al-Ḥārith, from ‘Alī, who said: “It is a duty upon every woman who wears a waistband to go out to the two Eids. He would not grant them permission to go out for anything except for the two Eids.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عُمَرَ، يَخْرُجُ إِلَى الْعِيدَيْنِ مَنِ اسْتَطَاعَ مِنْ أَهْلِهِ

english.narrator: Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Ayyūb → Nāfi‘ → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us, from Ayyūb, from Nāfi‘, who said: “‘Abd Allāh ibn ‘Umar used to take whoever he could from his family out to the two Eids.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ خَالِدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي قِلَابَةَ، قَالَ: قَالَتْ عَائِشَةُ: قَدْ كَانَتْ الْكَعَابُ تَخْرُجُ لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ مِنْ خِدْرِهَا فِي الْفِطْرِ وَالْأَضْحَى

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-A‘lā → Khālid → Abū Qilābah → ‘Ā’ishah
english.text: ‘Abd al-A‘lā narrated to us, from Khālid, from Abū Qilābah, who said: ‘Ā’ishah said: “The young girl with developing breasts used to come out from her secluded room for the Messenger of Allah ﷺ on Al-Fiṭr and Al-Aḍḥā.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سُلَيْمٍ عَنْ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، فِي قَوْلِهِ: {كَوَاعِبُ} [النبأ: 33] قَالَ: نَوَاهِدُ

english.narrator: Yaḥyā ibn Sulaym → Ibn Jurayj → Mujāhid
english.text: Yaḥyā ibn Sulaym narrated to us, from Ibn Jurayj, from Mujāhid regarding His saying: {Kawā‘ib} [An-Naba’: 33]. He said: “(Girls with) developing breasts.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبَّادُ بْنُ الْعَوَّامِ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ الْأَسْوَدِ، أَنَّ عَلْقَمَةَ، وَالْأَسْوَدَ كَانَا يُخْرِجَانِ نِسَاءَهُمَا فِي الْعِيدَيْنِ، وَيَمْنَعَانِهِنَّ مِنَ الْجُمُعَةِ

english.narrator: ‘Abbād ibn al-‘Awwām → Ḥajjāj → ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn al-Aswad → ‘Alqamah and Al-Aswad
english.text: ‘Abbād ibn al-‘Awwām narrated to us, from Ḥajjāj, from ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn al-Aswad, that ‘Alqamah and Al-Aswad used to take their women out on the two Eids, but prevented them from Friday prayer.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، قَالَ: إِنْ كَانَتْ امْرَأَةُ أَبِي مَيْسَرَةَ لَتَخْرُجَ إِلَى الْعِيدِ

english.narrator: Sharīk → Abū Isḥāq
english.text: Sharīk narrated to us, from Abū Isḥāq, who said: “Indeed, the wife of Abū Maysarah used to go out to the Eid.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: كَانَ لِعَلْقَمَةَ امْرَأَةٌ، فَدَخَلَتْ فِي السِّنِّ تَخْرُجُ إِلَى الْعِيدَيْنِ

english.narrator: Jarīr → Manṣūr → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Jarīr narrated to us, from Manṣūr, from Ibrāhīm, who said: “‘Alqamah had a wife who reached old age, and she used to go out to the two Eids.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ حَفْصَةَ، عَنْ أُمِّ عَطِيَّةَ، قَالَتْ: أَمَرَنَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَنْ نَخْرُجَ يَوْمَ الْفِطْرِ وَيَوْمَ النَّحْرِ قَالَتْ أُمُّ عَطِيَّةَ: فَقُلْنَا: أَرَأَيْتَ إِحْدَاهُنَّ لَا يَكُونُ لَهَا جِلْبَابٌ، قَالَ: فَتُلْبِسُهَا أُخْتُهَا مِنْ جِلْبَابِهَا

english.narrator: Abū Usāmah → Hishām → Ḥafṣah → Umm ‘Aṭiyyah
english.text: Abū Usāmah narrated to us, from Hishām, from Ḥafṣah, from Umm ‘Aṭiyyah, who said: “The Messenger of Allah ﷺ ordered us to go out on the day of Al-Fiṭr and the day of Naḥr.” Umm ‘Aṭiyyah said: We said: “What if one of us does not have a Jilbāb?” He said: “Then let her sister dress her from her Jilbāb.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: يُكْرَهُ خُرُوجُ النِّسَاءِ فِي الْعِيدَيْنِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Jarīr → Manṣūr → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Jarīr narrated to us, from Manṣūr, from Ibrāhīm, who said: “It is disliked for women to go out on the two Eids.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ أَنَّهُ كَانَ لَا يُخْرِجُ نِسَاءَهُ فِي الْعِيدَيْنِ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → ‘Abd Allāh ibn Jābir → Nāfi‘ → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Sufyān, from ‘Abd Allāh ibn Jābir, from Nāfi‘, from Ibn ‘Umar, that he used to not take his women out on the two Eids.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ لَا يَدَعُ امْرَأَةً مِنْ أَهْلِهِ تَخْرُجُ إِلَى فِطْرٍ، وَلَا إِلَى أَضْحَى

english.narrator: Abū Usāmah → Hishām ibn ‘Urwah → His Father
english.text: Abū Usāmah narrated to us, from Hishām ibn ‘Urwah, from his father, that he would not let any woman from his family go out to Fiṭr or Aḍḥā.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ، عَنْ قُرَّةَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ الْقَاسِمِ، قَالَ: كَانَ الْقَاسِمُ، أَشَدَّ شَيْءٍ عَلَى الْعَوَاتِقِ، لَا يَدَعُهُنَّ يَخْرُجْنَ فِي الْفِطْرِ وَالْأَضْحَى

english.narrator: Abū Dāwūd → Qurrah → ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn al-Qāsim → Al-Qāsim
english.text: Abū Dāwūd narrated to us, from Qurrah, who said: ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn al-Qāsim narrated to us, saying: “Al-Qāsim was very strict with young unmarried women; he would not let them go out on Fiṭr and Aḍḥā.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ حَسَنِ بْنِ صَالِحٍ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: كُرِهَ لِلشَّابَّةِ أَنْ تَخْرُجُ إِلَى الْعِيدَيْنِ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Ḥasan ibn Ṣāliḥ → Manṣūr → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Ḥasan ibn Ṣāliḥ, from Manṣūr, from Ibrāhīm, who said: “It was disliked for a young woman to go out to the two Eids.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ بْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ مُطَرِّفٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، قَالَ: يُصَلِّي أَرْبَعًا

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Sufyān ibn ‘Uyaynah → Muṭarrif → Al-Sha‘bī → ‘Abd Allāh
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Sufyān ibn ‘Uyaynah narrated to us, from Muṭarrif, from Al-Sha‘bī, from ‘Abd Allāh, who said: “He prays four rak‘ahs.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، وَحَفْصٌ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٌ، عَنْ مُسْلِمٍ، عَنْ مَسْرُوقٍ، قَالَ: قَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ: مَنْ فَاتَهُ الْعِيدُ فَلْيُصَلِّ أَرْبَعًا

english.narrator: Hushaym and Ḥafṣ → Ḥajjāj → Muslim → Masrūq → ‘Abd Allāh
english.text: Hushaym and Ḥafṣ narrated to us, from Ḥajjāj, from Muslim, from Masrūq, who said: ‘Abd Allāh said: “Whoever misses the Eid prayer, let him pray four rak‘ahs.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: يُصَلِّي أَرْبَعًا

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Ismā‘īl → Al-Sha‘bī
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Ismā‘īl, from Al-Sha‘bī, who said: “He prays four rak‘ahs.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، قَالَ: يُصَلِّي رَكْعَتَيْنِ وَيُكَبِّرُ

english.narrator: Yaḥyā ibn Sa‘īd → Ibn Jurayj → ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Yaḥyā ibn Sa‘īd narrated to us, from Ibn Jurayj, from ‘Aṭā’, who said: “He prays two rak‘ahs and recites Takbīr.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا بَعْضُ، آلِ أَنَسٍ، أَنَّ أَنَسًا كَانَ رُبَّمَا جَمَعَ أَهْلَهُ وَحَشَمَهُ يَوْمَ الْعِيدِ، فَصَلَّى بِهِمْ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ أَبِي عُتْبَةَ رَكْعَتَيْنِ

english.narrator: Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Yūnus → Some of Anas’s family → Anas
english.text: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us, from Yūnus, who said: Some of Anas’s family narrated to us, that Anas would sometimes gather his family and entourage on the day of Eid, and ‘Abd Allāh ibn Abī ‘Utbah would lead them in praying two rak‘ahs.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، قَالَ: كَانَ أَبُو عِيَاضٍ مُسْتَخْفِيًا، فَجَاءَهُ يَوْمَ عِيدٍ فَصَلَّى بِهِمْ رَكْعَتَيْنِ، وَدَعَا

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Shu‘bah → Al-Ḥakam → Abū ‘Iyāḍ
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Shu‘bah, from Al-Ḥakam, who said: “Abū ‘Iyāḍ was in hiding. The day of Eid came to him, so he led them in praying two rak‘ahs and supplicated.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ، عَنْ جُوَيْبِرٍ، عَنِ الضَّحَّاكِ، قَالَ: مَنْ كَانَ لَهُ عُذْرٌ يُعْذَرُ بِهِ فِي يَوْمِ فِطْرٍ أَوْ جُمُعَةٍ أَوْ أَضْحَى، فَصَلَاتُهُ أَرْبَعُ رَكَعَاتٍ

english.narrator: ‘Alī ibn Hāshim → Juwaybir → Al-Ḍaḥḥāk
english.text: ‘Alī ibn Hāshim narrated to us, from Juwaybir, from Al-Ḍaḥḥāk, who said: “Whoever has a valid excuse on the day of Fiṭr, Jumu‘ah, or Aḍḥā, his prayer is four rak‘ahs.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنْ أَبِي عُمَرَ، عَنِ ابْنِ الْحَنَفِيَّةِ، قَالَ: يُصَلِّي رَكْعَتَيْنِ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Ismā‘īl → Abū ‘Umar → Ibn al-Ḥanafiyyah
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Ismā‘īl, from Abū ‘Umar, from Ibn al-Ḥanafiyyah, who said: “He prays two rak‘ahs.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ رَبِيعٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: يُصَلِّي مِثْلَ صَلَاةِ الْإِمَامِ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Rabī‘ → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Rabī‘, from Al-Ḥasan, who said: “He prays similar to the Imam’s prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: إِذَا فَاتَتْكَ الصَّلَاةُ مَعَ الْإِمَامِ، فَصَلِّ مِثْلَ صَلَاتِهِ قَالَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ: وَإِذَا اسْتَقْبَلَ النَّاسُ رَاجِعِينَ، فَلْتَدْخُلْ أَدْنَى مَسْجِدٍ، ثُمَّ فَلْتُصَلِّ صَلَاةَ الْإِمَامِ، وَمَنْ لَا يَخْرُجُ إِلَى الْعِيدِ، فَلْيُصَلِّ مِثْلَ صَلَاةِ الْإِمَامِ

english.narrator: Jarīr → Mughīrah → Ḥammād → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Jarīr narrated to us, from Mughīrah, from Ḥammād, from Ibrāhīm, who said: “If you miss the prayer with the Imam, pray similar to his prayer.” Ibrāhīm said: “And if you encounter people returning, enter the nearest mosque, then pray the Imam’s prayer. And whoever does not go out to the Eid, let him pray similar to the Imam’s prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، فِي مَنْ لَمْ يُدْرِكِ الصَّلَاةَ يَوْمَ الْعِيدِ قَالَ: يُصَلِّي مِثْلَ صَلَاتِهِ، وَيُكَبِّرُ مِثْلَ تَكْبِيرِهِ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Mughīrah → Ḥammād
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, from Mughīrah, from Ḥammād, regarding the one who does not catch the prayer on the day of Eid. He said: “He prays similar to his prayer, and recites Takbīr similar to his Takbīr.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا إِسْحَاقَ، عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَجِيءُ يَوْمَ الْعِيدِ وَقَدْ فَرَغَ الْإِمَامُ، قَالَ: يُصَلِّي رَكْعَتَيْنِ

english.narrator: Sharīk → Abū Isḥāq
english.text: Sharīk narrated to us, saying: I asked Abū Isḥāq about a man who comes on the day of Eid when the Imam has finished. He said: “He prays two rak‘ahs.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ المُحَارِبِي، عَنْ ابْنِ عَوْنٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدٍ، فِي الَّذِي يَفُوتُهُ الْعِيدُ قَالَ: كَانَ يُسْتَحَبُّ أَنْ يُصَلِّيَ مِثْلَ صَلَاةِ الْإِمَامِ، وَإِنْ عَلِمَ مَا قَرَأَ بِهِ الْإِمَامُ، قَرَأَ بِهِ

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-Raḥmān al-Muḥāribī → Ibn ‘Awn → Muḥammad
english.text: ‘Abd al-Raḥmān al-Muḥāribī narrated to us, from Ibn ‘Awn, from Muḥammad, regarding the one who misses the Eid prayer. He said: “It was recommended that he prays similar to the Imam’s prayer, and if he knows what the Imam recited, he recites it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْحَمِيدِ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، قَالَ: إِذَا فَاتَتْكَ مِنْ صَلَاةِ الْعِيدِ رَكْعَةٌ، فَاقْضِهَا، وَاصْنَعْ فِيهَا مَا يَصْنَعُ الْإِمَامُ فِي الرَّكْعَةِ الْأُولَى

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Jarīr ibn ‘Abd al-Ḥamīd → Mughīrah → Ḥammād
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Jarīr ibn ‘Abd al-Ḥamīd narrated to us, from Mughīrah, from Ḥammād, who said: “If you miss a rak‘ah of the Eid prayer, make it up, and do in it what the Imam does in the first rak‘ah.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُعَاذُ بْنُ مُعَاذٍ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: يُكَبِّرُ مَعَهُ فِي هَذِهِ مَا أَدْرَكَ مِنْهَا، وَيَقْضِي الَّتِي فَاتَتْهُ، وَيُكَبِّرُ فِيهَا مِثْلَ تَكْبِيرِ الْإِمَامِ فِي الرَّكْعَةِ الثَّانِيَةِ

english.narrator: Mu‘ādh ibn Mu‘ādh → Ash‘ath → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Mu‘ādh ibn Mu‘ādh narrated to us, from Ash‘ath, from Al-Ḥasan, who said: “He recites Takbīr with him in this one what he catches of it, and makes up the one he missed, and recites Takbīr in it similar to the Imam’s Takbīr in the second rak‘ah.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، عَنْ حَنَشٍ، قَالَ: قِيلَ لِعَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ: إِنَّ ضَعَفَةً مِنْ ضَعَفَةِ النَّاسِ لَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ الْخُرُوجَ إِلَى الْجَبَّانَةِ فَأَمَرَ رَجُلًا يُصَلِّي بِالنَّاسِ أَرْبَعَ رَكَعَاتٍ، رَكْعَتَيْنِ لِلْعِيدِ، وَرَكْعَتَيْنِ لِمَكَانِ خُرُوجِهِمْ إِلَى الْجَبَّانَةِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ibn Idrīs → Layth → Al-Ḥakam → Ḥanash
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Idrīs narrated to us, from Layth, from Al-Ḥakam, from Ḥanash, who said: It was said to ‘Alī ibn Abī Ṭālib: “Some of the weak people cannot go out to the desert (open prayer area).” So he ordered a man to lead the people in praying four rak‘ahs; two rak‘ahs for the Eid, and two rak‘ahs in place of their going out to the desert.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، أَنَّ عَلِيًّا أَمَرَ رَجُلًا يُصَلِّي بِضَعَفَةِ النَّاسَ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ رَكْعَتَيْنِ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Abū Isḥāq → ‘Alī
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Sufyān, from Abū Isḥāq, that ‘Alī ordered a man to lead the weak people in praying two rak‘ahs in the mosque.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ أَبِي قَيْسٍ، قَالَ: أَظُنُّهُ عَنْ هُذَيْلٍ، أَنَّ عَلِيًّا أَمَرَ رَجُلًا يُصَلِّي بِضَعَفَةِ النَّاسِ يَوْمَ الْعِيدِ أَرْبَعًا كَصَلَاةِ الْهَجِيرِ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Abū Qays → Hudhayl → ‘Alī
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Sufyān, from Abū Qays, who said: I think he narrated from Hudhayl, that ‘Alī ordered a man to lead the weak people in praying four rak‘ahs on the day of Eid, like the midday (Hajīr) prayer.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ حَسَنِ بْنِ صَالِحٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، وَعَنْ عِيسَى، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، قَالَ: صَلَّى بِالنَّاسِ فِي مَسْجِدِ الْكُوفَةِ رَكْعَتَيْنِ فِي إِمَارَةِ مُصْعَبِ بْنِ الزُّبَيْرِ

english.narrator: Ḥumayd ibn ‘Abd al-Raḥmān → Ḥasan ibn Ṣāliḥ → Ibn Abī Laylā / ‘Īsā → ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn Abī Laylā
english.text: Ḥumayd ibn ‘Abd al-Raḥmān narrated to us, from Ḥasan ibn Ṣāliḥ, from Ibn Abī Laylā, and from ‘Īsā, from ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn Abī Laylā, who said: “He led the people in prayer in the Kufa mosque, praying two rak‘ahs during the emirate of Muṣ‘ab ibn al-Zubayr.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ حَسَنٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، أَنَّ عَلِيًّا أَمَرَ رَجُلًا يُصَلِّي بِالنَّاسِ فِي مَسْجِدِ الْكُوفَةِ رَكْعَتَيْنِ قَالَ: وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي لَيْلَى: يُصَلِّي رَكْعَتَيْنِ فَقَالَ رَجُلٌ لِابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى: يُصَلِّي بِغَيْرِ خُطْبَةٍ؟ قَالَ: نَعَمْ

english.narrator: Ḥumayd ibn ‘Abd al-Raḥmān → Ḥasan → Ibn Abī Laylā → ‘Alī
english.text: Ḥumayd ibn ‘Abd al-Raḥmān narrated to us, from Ḥasan, from Ibn Abī Laylā, that ‘Alī ordered a man to lead the people in praying two rak‘ahs in the Kufa mosque. He said: Ibn Abī Laylā said: “He prays two rak‘ahs.” A man said to Ibn Abī Laylā: “Does he pray without a sermon?” He said: “Yes.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُسْلِمُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ بْنِ الْمَذْكُورِ الْخَارِقِيِّ، قَالَ: صَلَّى بِنَا الْقَاسِمُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ يَوْمَ عِيدٍ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ الْجَامِعِ رَكْعَتَيْنِ وَخَطَبَ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Muslim ibn Yazīd ibn al-Madhkūr al-Khāriqī → Al-Qāsim ibn ‘Abd al-Raḥmān
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: Muslim ibn Yazīd ibn al-Madhkūr al-Khāriqī narrated to us, saying: “Al-Qāsim ibn ‘Abd al-Raḥmān led us in prayer on the day of Eid in the grand mosque, praying two rak‘ahs, and he delivered a sermon.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَرْوَانُ بْنُ مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنْ عُرَيْفِ بْنِ دِرْهَمٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ أَبِي هُذَيْلٍ، يَأْتِي الْمَسْجِدَ الْأَعْظَمَ يَوْمَ الْعِيدِ

english.narrator: Marwān ibn Mu‘āwiyah → ‘Urayf ibn Dirham → ‘Abd Allāh ibn Abī Hudhayl
english.text: Marwān ibn Mu‘āwiyah narrated to us, from ‘Urayf ibn Dirham, who said: “I saw ‘Abd Allāh ibn Abī Hudhayl coming to the grand mosque on the day of Eid.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ بُرْدٍ، عَنْ مَكْحُولٍ، قَالَ: يُكَبِّرُ، ثُمَّ يَقُومُ فَيَقْضِي، ثُمَّ يُكَبِّرُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → ‘Abd al-A‘lā → Burd → Makḥūl
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Abd al-A‘lā narrated to us, from Burd, from Makḥūl, who said: “He recites Takbīr, then stands up and makes up [the missed part], then recites Takbīr.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ شُبْرُمَةَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: يَقْضِي، ثُمَّ يُكَبِّرُ

english.narrator: Muḥammad ibn Fuḍayl → Ibn Shubrumah → Al-Sha‘bī
english.text: Muḥammad ibn Fuḍayl narrated to us, from Ibn Shubrumah, from Al-Sha‘bī, who said: “He makes up [the prayer], then recites Takbīr.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ ابْنَ شُبْرُمَةَ، غَيْرَ مَرَّةٍ إِذَا فَاتَهُ شَيْءٌ مِنَ الصَّلَاةِ أَيَّامَ التَّشْرِيقِ، قَامَ فَقَضَى، ثُمَّ كَبَّرَ

english.narrator: Muḥammad ibn Fuḍayl → Ibn Shubrumah
english.text: Muḥammad ibn Fuḍayl said: “I saw Ibn Shubrumah more than once; when he missed part of the prayer during the days of Tashrīq, he stood up, made it up, then recited Takbīr.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: "كَانَ يَقُولُ: يُكَبِّرُ مَعَهُ، ثُمَّ يَقُومُ فَيَقْضِي

english.narrator: Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Yūnus → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us, from Yūnus, from Al-Ḥasan, who said: “He used to say: He recites Takbīr with him, then stands up and makes up [the prayer].”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، وَابْنِ سِيرِينَ، فِي الرَّجُلِ تَفُوتُهُ الرَّكْعَةُ أَيَّامَ التَّشْرِيقِ، قَالَ ابْنُ سِيرِينَ: يَقْضِي، ثُمَّ يُكَبِّرُ وَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ: يُكَبِّرُ، ثُمَّ يَقْضِي، وَقَالَ الْحَكَمُ: يُكَبِّرُ، ثُمَّ يَقْضِي

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → ‘Abd Allāh ibn Idrīs → Hishām → Al-Ḥasan and Ibn Sīrīn / Al-Ḥakam
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Abd Allāh ibn Idrīs narrated to us, from Hishām, from Al-Ḥasan and Ibn Sīrīn, regarding a man who misses a rak‘ah during the days of Tashrīq. Ibn Sīrīn said: “He makes up [the rak‘ah], then recites Takbīr.” Al-Ḥasan said: “He recites Takbīr, then makes up [the rak‘ah].” And Al-Ḥakam said: “He recites Takbīr, then makes up [the rak‘ah].”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عِيسَى بْنُ يُونُسَ، عَنْ أَبِي حَنِيفَةَ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: إِذَا فَاتَتْكَ رَكْعَةٌ أَيَّامَ التَّشْرِيقِ، فَلَا تُكَبِّرْ حَتَّى تَقْضِيَهَا

english.narrator: ‘Īsā ibn Yūnus → Abū Ḥanīfah → Ḥammād → Ibrāhīm
english.text: ‘Īsā ibn Yūnus narrated to us, from Abū Ḥanīfah, from Ḥammād, from Ibrāhīm, who said: “If you miss a rak‘ah during the days of Tashrīq, do not recite Takbīr until you make it up.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، قَالَ: أنا يُونُسُ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: يُكَبِّرُ مَعَ الْإِمَامِ، ثُمَّ يَقْضِي مَا سَبَقَ بِهِ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Yūnus → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, saying: Yūnus informed us, from Al-Ḥasan, who said: “He recites Takbīr with the Imam, then makes up what he missed.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ سِيرِينَ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَقُولُ: يَقْضِي مَا فَاتَهُ، ثُمَّ يُكَبِّرُ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Hishām → Ibn Sīrīn
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, from Hishām, from Ibn Sīrīn, that he used to say: “He makes up what he missed, then recites Takbīr.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَكَّامٌ الرَّازِي، عَنْ عَنْبَسَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْكَرِيمِ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، قَالَ: يُكَبِّرُ مَعَ الْإِمَامِ، ثُمَّ يُكَبِّرُ إِذَا قَضَى قَالَ أَبُو بَكْرٍ: وَبَلَغَنِي أَنَّ هَذَا قَوْلُ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى

english.narrator: Ḥakkām al-Rāzī → ‘Anbasah → Ibn Abī Laylā → ‘Abd al-Karīm → Mujāhid
english.text: Ḥakkām al-Rāzī narrated to us, from ‘Anbasah, from Ibn Abī Laylā, from ‘Abd al-Karīm, from Mujāhid, who said: “He recites Takbīr with the Imam, then recites Takbīr when he finishes making up [the prayer].” Abū Bakr said: It reached me that this is the view of Ibn Abī Laylā.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصُ بْنُ غِيَاثٍ، عَنْ عَمْرٍو، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: إِذَا صَلَّى وَحْدَهُ، أَوْ فِي جَمَاعَةٍ، أَوْ تَطَوَّعَ كَبَّرَ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ḥafṣ ibn Ghiyāth → ‘Amr → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ḥafṣ ibn Ghiyāth narrated to us, from ‘Amr, from Al-Ḥasan, who said: “If he prays alone, or in congregation, or voluntarily, he recites Takbīr.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ أَبِي حَنِيفَةَ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: لَا يُكَبِّرُ إِلَّا أَنْ يُصَلِّيَ فِي جَمَاعَةٍ

english.narrator: Ḥafṣ → Abū Ḥanīfah → Ḥammād → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Ḥafṣ narrated to us, from Abū Ḥanīfah, from Ḥammād, from Ibrāhīm, who said: “He does not recite Takbīr unless he prays in congregation.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ هَمَّامٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ قَتَادَةَ، صَلَّى وَحْدَهُ أَيَّامَ التَّشْرِيقِ، فَكَبَّرَ

english.narrator: Ibn Mahdī → Hammām → Qatādah
english.text: Ibn Mahdī narrated to us, from Hammām, who said: “I saw Qatādah praying alone during the days of Tashrīq, and he recited Takbīr.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: كَبِّرْ فِي التَّطَوُّعِ، وَإِنْ صَلَّيْتَ وَحْدَكَ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Jābir → Al-Sha‘bī
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Sufyān, from Jābir, from Al-Sha‘bī, who said: “Recite Takbīr in voluntary prayers, even if you pray alone.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَكَّامٌ الرَّازِي، عَنْ عَنْبَسَةَ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، قَالَ: التَّكْبِيرُ أَيَّامَ التَّشْرِيقِ فِي كُلِّ نَافِلَةٍ وَفَرِيضَةٍ

english.narrator: Ḥakkām al-Rāzī → ‘Anbasah → Layth → Mujāhid
english.text: Ḥakkām al-Rāzī narrated to us, from ‘Anbasah, from Layth, from Mujāhid, who said: “Takbīr during the days of Tashrīq is in every voluntary and obligatory prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ مُسْهِرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْكَرِيمِ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، قَالَ: كَانُوا يُكَبِّرُونَ فِي دُبُرِ الرَّكْعَتَيْنِ يَوْمَ النَّحْرِ

english.narrator: ‘Alī ibn Mushir → Ibn Abī Laylā → ‘Abd al-Karīm → Mujāhid
english.text: ‘Alī ibn Mushir narrated to us, from Ibn Abī Laylā, from ‘Abd al-Karīm, from Mujāhid, who said: “They used to recite Takbīr after the two rak‘ahs on the day of Naḥr.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو خَالِدٍ الْأَحْمَرُ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْحَمِيدِ بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ، عَنْ وَهْبِ بْنِ كَيْسَانَ، قَالَ: اجْتَمَعَ عِيدَانِ فِي عَهْدِ ابْنِ الزُّبَيْرِ فَأَخَّرَ الْخُرُوجَ، ثُمَّ خَرَجَ فَخَطَبَ، فَأَطَالَ الْخُطْبَةَ، ثُمَّ صَلَّى وَلَمْ يَخْرُجْ إِلَى الْجُمُعَةِ، فَعَابَ ذَلِكَ أُنَاسٌ عَلَيْهِ، فَبَلَغَ ذَلِكَ عِنْدَ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ فَقَالَ: أَصَابَ السُّنَّةَ فَبَلَغَ ابْنَ الزُّبَيْرِ، فَقَالَ: شَهِدْتُ الْعِيدَ مَعَ عُمَرَ، فَصَنَعَ كَمَا صَنَعْتُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Abū Khālid al-Aḥmar → ‘Abd al-Ḥamīd ibn Ja‘far → Wahb ibn Kaysān
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abū Khālid al-Aḥmar narrated to us, from ‘Abd al-Ḥamīd ibn Ja‘far, from Wahb ibn Kaysān, who said: “Two Eids coincided during the time of Ibn al-Zubayr. He delayed coming out, then he came out and delivered a sermon, prolonging it, then he prayed and did not come out for the Friday prayer. Some people criticized him for that. This reached Ibn ‘Abbās, and he said: ‘He followed the Sunnah.’ This reached Ibn al-Zubayr, and he said: ‘I witnessed the Eid with ‘Umar, and he did as I did.’”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ بْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، عَنْ أَبِي عُبَيْدٍ، مَوْلَى ابْنِ أَزْهَرَ، قَالَ: شَهِدْتُ الْعِيدَ مَعَ عُثْمَانَ، وَوَافَقَ يَوْمَ جُمُعَةٍ، فَقَالَ: إِنَّ هَذَا يَوْمٌ اجْتَمَعَ فِيهِ عِيدَانِ لِلْمُسْلِمِينَ، فَمَنْ كَانَ هَاهُنَا مِنْ أَهْلِ الْعَوَالِي فَقَدْ آذَنَّا لَهُ أَنْ يَنْصَرِفَ، وَمَنْ أَحَبَّ أَنْ يَمْكُثَ فَلْيَمْكُثْ

english.narrator: Sufyān ibn ‘Uyaynah → Al-Zuhrī → Abū ‘Ubayd
english.text: Sufyān ibn ‘Uyaynah narrated to us, from Al-Zuhrī, from Abū ‘Ubayd, the freed slave of Ibn Azhar, who said: I witnessed the Eid with ‘Uthmān, and it coincided with a Friday. He said: “This is a day in which two Eids have come together for the Muslims. Whoever is here from the people of Al-‘Awālī, we have permitted him to leave, and whoever wishes to stay, let him stay.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، قَالَ: اجْتَمَعَ عِيدَانِ عَلَى عَهْدِ عَلِيٍّ، فَصَلَّى بِالنَّاسِ، ثُمَّ خَطَبَ عَلَى رَاحِلَتِهِ فَقَالَ: يَا أَيَّهَا النَّاسُ، مَنْ شَهِدَ مِنْكُمُ الْعِيدَ فَقَدْ قَضَى جُمُعَتَهُ إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ

english.narrator: Abū al-Aḥwaṣ → ‘Abd al-A‘lā → Abū ‘Abd al-Raḥmān
english.text: Abū al-Aḥwaṣ narrated to us, from ‘Abd al-A‘lā, from Abū ‘Abd al-Raḥmān, who said: Two Eids coincided during the time of ‘Alī. He led the people in prayer, then delivered a sermon on his mount, saying: “O people, whoever among you attended the Eid has fulfilled his Friday prayer, if Allah wills.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصُ بْنُ غِيَاثٍ، عَنْ جَعْفَرٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، قَالَ: اجْتَمَعَ عِيدَانِ عَلَى عَهْدِ عَلِيٍّ فَشَهِدَ بِهِمِ الْعِيدَ، ثُمَّ قَالَ: إِنَّا مُجَمِّعُونَ، فَمَنْ أَرَادَ أَنْ يَشْهَدَ، فَلْيَشْهَدْ

english.narrator: Ḥafṣ ibn Ghiyāth → Ja‘far → His Father
english.text: Ḥafṣ ibn Ghiyāth narrated to us, from Ja‘far, from his father, who said: Two Eids coincided during the time of ‘Alī. He attended the Eid with them, then said: “We are going to hold Friday prayer, so whoever wants to attend, let him attend.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْحَمِيدِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْمُنْتَشِرِ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ حَبِيبِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ، عَنِ النُّعْمَانِ بْنِ بَشِيرٍ، أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ كَانَ يَقْرَأُ فِي الْعِيدَيْنِ بِسَبِّحِ اسْمَ رَبِّكَ الْأَعْلَى، وَهَلْ أَتَاكَ حَدِيثُ الْغَاشِيَةِ، وَإِذَا اجْتَمَعَ الْعِيدَانِ فِي يَوْمٍ، قَرَأَ بِهِمَا فِيهِمَا

english.narrator: Jarīr ibn ‘Abd al-Ḥamīd → Ibrāhīm ibn Muḥammad ibn al-Muntashir → His Father → Ḥabīb ibn Sālim → Al-Nu‘mān ibn Bashīr
english.text: Jarīr ibn ‘Abd al-Ḥamīd narrated to us, from Ibrāhīm ibn Muḥammad ibn al-Muntashir, from his father, from Ḥabīb ibn Sālim, from Al-Nu‘mān ibn Bashīr, that the Prophet ﷺ used to recite in the two Eids: ‘Glorify the Name of your Lord, the Most High’ [Al-A’la] and ‘Has there come to you the report of the Overwhelming Event?’ [Al-Ghashiyah]. And if the two Eids coincided in one day, he would recite them in both.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ وَهْبِ بْنِ كَيْسَانَ، قَالَ: اجْتَمَعَ عِيدَانِ فِي يَوْمٍ، فَخَرَجَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ الزُّبَيْرِ فَصَلَّى الْعِيدَ بَعْدَمَا ارْتَفَعَ النَّهَارُ، ثُمَّ دَخَلَ فَلَمْ يَخْرُجْ حَتَّى صَلَّى الْعَصْرَ قَالَ هِشَامٌ: فَذَكَرْتُ ذَلِكَ لِنَافِعٍ، أَوْ ذُكِرَ لَهُ فَقَالَ: ذُكِرَ ذَلِكَ لِابْنِ عُمَرَ، فَلَمْ يُنْكِرْهُ

english.narrator: Abū Usāmah → Hishām ibn ‘Urwah → Wahb ibn Kaysān
english.text: Abū Usāmah narrated to us, from Hishām ibn ‘Urwah, from Wahb ibn Kaysān, who said: “Two Eids coincided in one day. ‘Abd Allāh ibn al-Zubayr went out and prayed the Eid after the day had risen, then he entered and did not come out until he prayed ‘Aṣr.” Hishām said: I mentioned that to Nāfi‘, or it was mentioned to him, and he said: “That was mentioned to Ibn ‘Umar, and he did not disapprove of it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، قَالَ: اجْتَمَعَ عِيدَانِ فِي عَهْدِ ابْنِ الزُّبَيْرِ، فَصَلَّى بِهِمِ الْعِيدَ، ثُمَّ صَلَّى بِهِمِ الْجُمُعَةَ صَلَاةَ الظُّهْرِ أَرْبَعًا

english.narrator: Hushaym → Manṣūr → ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, from Manṣūr, from ‘Aṭā’, who said: “Two Eids coincided during the time of Ibn al-Zubayr. He led them in the Eid prayer, then he led them in the Friday prayer as Ẓuhr prayer, four rak‘ahs.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ عَطَاءِ بْنِ السَّائِبِ، قَالَ: اجْتَمَعَ عِيدَانِ عَلَى عَهْدِ الْحَجَّاجِ، فَصَلَّى أَحَدَهُمَا فَقَالَ أَبُو الْبَخْتَرِيِّ: قَاتَلَهُ اللَّهُ، أَنَّى عَلِقَ هَذَا

english.narrator: Hushaym → ‘Aṭā’ ibn al-Sā’ib
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, from ‘Aṭā’ ibn al-Sā’ib, who said: “Two Eids coincided during the time of Al-Ḥajjāj, so he prayed one of them.” Abū al-Bakhtarī said: “May Allah fight him, from where did he get this?”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: يُجْزِيهِ الْأُولَى مِنْهُمَا

english.narrator: Hushaym → Shu‘bah → Al-Ḥakam → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, from Shu‘bah, from Al-Ḥakam, from Ibrāhīm, who said: “The first of the two suffices him.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُعْتَمِرٌ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، قَالَ: إِذَا اجْتَمَعَ عِيدَانِ فِي يَوْمٍ، فَأَيُّهُمَا أَتَيْتُ أَجْزَاكَ

english.narrator: Mu‘tamir → Layth → ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Mu‘tamir narrated to us, from Layth, from ‘Aṭā’, who said: “If two Eids coincide in one day, whichever one you attend suffices you.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ نُمَيْرٍ، عَنْ إِسْرَائِيلَ، عَنْ عُثْمَانَ الثَّقَفِيِّ، عَنْ أَبِي رَمْلَةَ الشَّامِيِّ، قَالَ: شَهِدْتُ مُعَاوِيَةَ يَسْأَلُ زَيْدَ بْنَ أَرْقَمَ: هَلْ شَهِدْتَ مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ عِيدَيْنِ اجْتَمَعَا؟ قَالَ: نَعَمْ، قَالَ: فَكَيْفَ صَنَعَ؟ قَالَ: صَلَّى الْعِيدَ، ثُمَّ رَخَّصَ فِي الْجُمُعَةِ قَالَ: مَنْ شَاءَ أَنْ يُصَلِّيَ فَلْيُصَلِّ

english.narrator: ‘Abd Allāh ibn Numayr → Isrā’īl → ‘Uthmān al-Thaqafī → Abū Ramlah al-Shāmī
english.text: ‘Abd Allāh ibn Numayr narrated to us, from Isrā’īl, from ‘Uthmān al-Thaqafī, from Abū Ramlah al-Shāmī, who said: I witnessed Mu‘āwiyah asking Zayd ibn Arqam: “Did you witness two Eids coinciding with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ?” He said: “Yes.” He said: “What did he do?” He said: “He prayed the Eid, then granted a concession regarding the Friday prayer, saying: ‘Whoever wishes to pray, let him pray.’”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءِ بْنِ السَّائِبِ، قَالَ: اجْتَمَعَ الْعِيدَانِ فِي يَوْمٍ، فَقَامَ الْحَجَّاجُ فِي الْعِيدِ الْأَوَّلِ، فَقَالَ: مَنْ شَاءَ أَنْ يُجَمِّعَ مَعَنَا، فَلْيُجَمِّعْ، وَمَنْ شَاءَ أَنْ يَنْصَرِفَ، فَلْيَنْصَرِفْ وَلَا حَرَجَ فَقَالَ أَبُو الْبَخْتَرِيِّ، وَمَيْسَرَةُ: مَا لَهُ قَاتَلَهُ اللَّهُ، مِنْ أَيْنَ سَقَطَ عَلَى هَذَا؟

english.narrator: Muḥammad ibn Fuḍayl → ‘Aṭā’ ibn al-Sā’ib
english.text: Muḥammad ibn Fuḍayl narrated to us, from ‘Aṭā’ ibn al-Sā’ib, who said: Two Eids coincided in one day. Al-Ḥajjāj stood up during the first Eid and said: “Whoever wishes to attend the Friday prayer with us, let him do so; and whoever wishes to leave, let him leave, and there is no blame.” Abū al-Bakhtarī and Maysarah said: “What is wrong with him, may Allah fight him? From where did he stumble upon this?”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، قَالَ: أنا الْحَجَّاجُ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، عَنْ يَعْلَى، وَعَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، فِي الْعِيدَيْنِ إِذَا اجْتَمَعَا قَالَ: يُجْزِئُ أَحَدُهُمَا

english.narrator: Yazīd ibn Hārūn → Al-Ḥajjāj → ‘Aṭā’ → Ya‘lā / Shu‘bah → Al-Ḥakam → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Yazīd ibn Hārūn narrated to us, saying: Al-Ḥajjāj informed us, from ‘Aṭā’, from Ya‘lā, and from Shu‘bah, from Al-Ḥakam, from Ibrāhīm, regarding the two Eids if they coincide. He said: “One of them suffices.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا حَجَّاجٌ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ بْنِ رُفَيْعٍ، عَنِ الزُّبَيْرِ، قَالَ: يُجْزِئُ أَحَدُهُمَا

english.narrator: Yazīd ibn Hārūn → Ḥajjāj → ‘Abd al-‘Azīz ibn Rufay‘ → Al-Zubayr
english.text: Yazīd ibn Hārūn narrated to us, saying: Ḥajjāj informed us, from ‘Abd al-‘Azīz ibn Rufay‘, from Al-Zubayr, who said: “One of them suffices.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُعَاوِيَةُ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، قَالَ: نَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ مُجَالِدٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: إِذَا كَانَ يَوْمَ جُمُعَةٍ، وَعِيدٍ أَجْزَأَ أَحَدُهُمَا مِنَ الْآخَرِ

english.narrator: Mu‘āwiyah → Hishām → Sufyān → Mujālid → Al-Sha‘bī
english.text: Mu‘āwiyah narrated to us, from Hishām, who said: Sufyān narrated to us, from Mujālid, from Al-Sha‘bī, who said: “If it is a Friday and an Eid, one of them suffices for the other.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ زُبَيْدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنْ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: صَلَاةُ السَّفَرِ رَكْعَتَانِ، وَالْجُمُعَةُ رَكْعَتَانِ، وَالْعِيدَانِ رَكْعَتَانِ، تَمَامٌ غَيْرُ قَصْرٍ، عَلَى لِسَانِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Sharīk → Zubayd → Ibn Abī Laylā → ‘Umar
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Sharīk narrated to us, from Zubayd, from Ibn Abī Laylā, from ‘Umar, who said: “The prayer of travel is two rak‘ahs, Friday prayer is two rak‘ahs, and the two Eids are two rak‘ahs; complete, not shortened, according to the tongue of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ عَدِيِّ بْنِ ثَابِتٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: خَرَجَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَوْمَ عِيدِ فِطْرٍ أَوْ أَضْحَى، فَصَلَّى بِالنَّاسِ رَكْعَتَيْنِ، ثُمَّ انْصَرَفَ وَلَمْ يُصَلِّ قَبْلَهَا وَلَا بَعْدَهَا

english.narrator: Ibn Idrīs → Shu‘bah → ‘Adī ibn Thābit → Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr → Ibn ‘Abbās
english.text: Ibn Idrīs narrated to us, from Shu‘bah, from ‘Adī ibn Thābit, from Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr, from Ibn ‘Abbās, who said: “The Messenger of Allah ﷺ went out on the day of Eid Fiṭr or Aḍḥā, and led the people in praying two rak‘ahs, then he left and did not pray before it or after it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ نُمَيْرٍ، عَنْ دَاوُدَ بْنِ قَيْسٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي عِيَاضُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي سَرْحٍ، عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَخْرُجُ يَوْمَ الْعِيدِ يَوْمَ الْفِطْرِ، فَيُصَلِّي بِالنَّاسِ رَكْعَتَيْنِ، ثُمَّ يُسَلِّمُ

english.narrator: Ibn Numayr → Dāwūd ibn Qays → ‘Iyāḍ ibn ‘Abd Allāh ibn Abī Sarḥ → Abū Sa‘īd
english.text: Ibn Numayr narrated to us, from Dāwūd ibn Qays, who said: ‘Iyāḍ ibn ‘Abd Allāh ibn Abī Sarḥ narrated to me, from Abū Sa‘īd, who said: “The Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to go out on the day of Eid, the day of Fiṭr, and lead the people in praying two rak‘ahs, then say Taslīm.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ دَاوُدَ بْنِ قَيْسٍ، عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ، قَالَ: إِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ خَطَبَ يَوْمَ عِيدٍ عَلَى رَاحِلَتِهِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Wakī‘ → Dāwūd ibn Qays → Abū Sa‘īd
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Dāwūd ibn Qays, from Abū Sa‘īd, who said: “The Messenger of Allah ﷺ delivered a sermon on the day of Eid on his mount.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ حُصَيْنٍ، عَنْ مَيْسَرَةَ أَبِي جَمِيلَةَ، قَالَ: شَهِدْتُ مَعَ عَلِيٍّ الْعِيدَ، فَلَمَّا صَلَّى خَطَبَ عَلَى رَاحِلَتِهِ، قَالَ: وَكَانَ عُثْمَانُ، يَفْعَلُهُ

english.narrator: Ibn Idrīs → Ḥuṣayn → Maysarah Abū Jamīlah
english.text: Ibn Idrīs narrated to us, from Ḥuṣayn, from Maysarah Abū Jamīlah, who said: “I witnessed the Eid with ‘Alī. When he prayed, he delivered the sermon on his mount.” He said: “And ‘Uthmān used to do that.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ عَيَّاشٍ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، قَالَ: صَلَّى بِنَا عَلِيٌّ الْعِيدَ، ثُمَّ خَطَبَ عَلَى رَاحِلَتِهِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr ibn ‘Ayyāsh → Yazīd → Ibn Abī Laylā
english.text: Abū Bakr ibn ‘Ayyāsh narrated to us, from Yazīd, from Ibn Abī Laylā, who said: “‘Alī led us in the Eid prayer, then he delivered the sermon on his mount.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عُبَيْدٍ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ أَبِي خَالِدٍ، عَنْ قَيْسٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ الْمُغِيرَةَ بْنَ شُعْبَةَ يَخْطُبُ عَلَى نَجِيبَةٍ

english.narrator: Muḥammad ibn ‘Ubayd → Ismā‘īl ibn Abī Khālid → Qays
english.text: Muḥammad ibn ‘Ubayd narrated to us, from Ismā‘īl ibn Abī Khālid, from Qays, who said: “I saw Al-Mughīrah ibn Shu‘bah delivering a sermon on a she-camel.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ أَبِي خَالِدٍ، عَنْ أَخِيهِ، عَنْ أَبِي كَاهِلٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَخْطُبُ عَلَى نَاقَةٍ حَزْمَى، وَحَبَشِيٌّ مُمْسِكٌ بِخِطَامِهَا

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Ismā‘īl ibn Abī Khālid → His Brother → Abū Kāhil
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Ismā‘īl ibn Abī Khālid, from his brother, from Abū Kāhil, who said: “I saw the Prophet ﷺ delivering a sermon on a firm she-camel, while an Abyssinian was holding its reins.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، قَالَ: أنا سَعِيدُ بْنُ أَبِي عَرُوبَةَ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ شَهْرِ بْنِ حَوْشَبٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ غنم، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ خَارِجَةَ، أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ خَطَبَهُمْ وَهُوَ عَلَى رَاحِلَتِهِ قَالَ: وَإِنَّ رَاحِلَتَهُ لَتُصَعْصِعُ بِمَرَّتِهَا، وَإِنَّ لُعَابَهَا لَيَسِيلُ بَيْنَ كَتِفَيَّ

english.narrator: Yazīd ibn Hārūn → Sa‘īd ibn Abī ‘Arūbah → Qatādah → Shahr ibn Ḥawshab → ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn Ghanam → ‘Amr ibn Khārijah
english.text: Yazīd ibn Hārūn narrated to us, saying: Sa‘īd ibn Abī ‘Arūbah informed us, from Qatādah, from Shahr ibn Ḥawshab, from ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn Ghanam, from ‘Amr ibn Khārijah, that the Prophet ﷺ addressed them while he was on his mount. He said: “And his mount was ruminating on its cud, and its saliva was flowing between my shoulders.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هَاشِمُ بْنُ الْقَاسِمِ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ، قَالَ: نَا الْهِرْمَاسُ بْنُ زِيَادٍ، قَالَ: كُنْتُ رِدْفًا إِلَى يَوْمِ الْأَضْحَى، وَرَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَخْطُبُ عَلَى نَاقَةٍ بِمِنًى

english.narrator: Hāshim ibn al-Qāsim → ‘Ikrimah ibn ‘Ammār → Al-Hirmās ibn Ziyād
english.text: Hāshim ibn al-Qāsim narrated to us, from ‘Ikrimah ibn ‘Ammār, who said: Al-Hirmās ibn Ziyād narrated to us, saying: “I was riding pillion to the day of Aḍḥā, and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was delivering a sermon on a she-camel in Mina.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ أَبِي حُبَابٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنِ الْمُغِيرَةِ بْنِ شُعْبَةَ، قَالَ: خَطَبَنَا عَلِيٌّ يَوْمَ عِيدٍ عَلَى رَاحِلَتِهِ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Abū Ḥubāb → His Father → Al-Mughīrah ibn Shu‘bah
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Abū Ḥubāb, from his father, from Al-Mughīrah ibn Shu‘bah, who said: “‘Alī addressed us on the day of Eid on his mount.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ شِبَاكٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: كَانَ الْإِمَامُ يَوْمَ الْعِيدِ يَبْدَأُ فَيُصَلِّي، ثُمَّ يَرْكَبُ فَيَخْطُبُ

english.narrator: Jarīr → Mughīrah → Shibāk → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Jarīr narrated to us, from Mughīrah, from Shibāk, from Ibrāhīm, who said: “The Imam on the day of Eid would start by praying, then he would mount and deliver the sermon.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا الْمُحَارِبِيُّ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ بْنِ عُمَيْرٍ، أَنَّهُ سَمِعَهُ يَذْكُرُ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ الْمُغِيرَةَ بْنَ شُعْبَةَ، يَخْطُبُ النَّاسَ يَوْمَ الْعِيدِ عَلَى بَعِيرٍ

english.narrator: Al-Muḥāribī → ‘Abd al-Malik ibn ‘Umayr
english.text: Al-Muḥāribī narrated to us, from ‘Abd al-Malik ibn ‘Umayr, that he heard him mentioning, saying: “I saw Al-Mughīrah ibn Shu‘bah addressing the people on the day of Eid on a camel.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: كَانَ يَجِبُ لِلنِّسَاءِ أَنْ يُكَبِّرْنَ دُبُرَ الصَّلَاةِ أَيَّامَ التَّشْرِيقِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Hushaym → Mughīrah → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hushaym narrated to us, from Mughīrah, from Ibrāhīm, who said: “It was obligatory for women to recite Takbīr after the prayer during the days of Tashrīq.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي عَدِيٍّ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، قَالَ: كَانَ الْحَسَنُ، لَا يَرَى التَّكْبِيرَ عَلَى النِّسَاءِ أَيَّامَ التَّشْرِيقِ

english.narrator: Muḥammad ibn Abī ‘Adī → Ash‘ath → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Muḥammad ibn Abī ‘Adī narrated to us, from Ash‘ath, who said: “Al-Ḥasan did not consider Takbīr obligatory for women during the days of Tashrīq.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الْقَارِي، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُتْبَةَ، قَالَ: مِنَ السُّنَّةِ أَنْ يُكَبِّرَ الْإِمَامُ عَلَى الْمِنْبَرِ، عَلَى الْعِيدَيْنِ، تِسْعًا قَبْلَ الْخُطْبَةِ، وَسَبْعًا بَعْدَهَا

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Muḥammad ibn ‘Abd al-Raḥmān al-Qārī → ‘Ubayd Allāh ibn ‘Abd Allāh ibn ‘Utbah
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Sufyān, from Muḥammad ibn ‘Abd al-Raḥmān al-Qārī, from ‘Ubayd Allāh ibn ‘Abd Allāh ibn ‘Utbah, who said: “It is from the Sunnah that the Imam recites Takbīr on the pulpit during the two Eids: nine times before the sermon and seven times after it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ الطَّيَالِسِيُّ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ أَبِي الْحَسْنَاءِ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: يُكَبَّرُ عَلَى الْمِنْبَرِ يَوْمَ الْعِيدَيْنِ أَرْبَعُ عَشْرَةَ تَكْبِيرَةً

english.narrator: Abū Dāwūd al-Ṭayālisī → Al-Ḥasan ibn Abī al-Ḥasnā’ → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Abū Dāwūd al-Ṭayālisī narrated to us, from Al-Ḥasan ibn Abī al-Ḥasnā’, from Al-Ḥasan, who said: “Fourteen Takbīrs are recited on the pulpit on the day of the two Eids.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عُبَيْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مُوسَى، عَنْ بُكَيْرِ بْنِ عَامِرٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: يَتَيَمَّمُ لِلْعِيدَيْنِ وَالْجِنَازَةِ

english.narrator: ‘Ubayd Allāh ibn Mūsā → Bukayr ibn ‘Āmir → Ibrāhīm
english.text: ‘Ubayd Allāh ibn Mūsā narrated to us, from Bukayr ibn ‘Āmir, from Ibrāhīm, who said: “He performs Tayammum for the two Eids and the funeral.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ نَجِيحٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ الْقَاسِمِ، فِي الرَّجُلِ يُحْدِثُ فِي الْعِيدِ، وَيَخَافُ الْفَوْتَ قَالَ: يَتَيَمَّمُ وَيُصَلِّي إِذَا خَافَ

english.narrator: Ḥumayd ibn ‘Abd al-Raḥmān → His Father → Ja‘far ibn Najīḥ → ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn al-Qāsim
english.text: Ḥumayd ibn ‘Abd al-Raḥmān narrated to us, from his father, from Ja‘far ibn Najīḥ, from ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn al-Qāsim, regarding a man who invalidates his wudu on the day of Eid and fears missing [the prayer]. He said: “He performs Tayammum and prays if he fears [missing it].”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُعَاذُ بْنُ مُعَاذٍ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، فِي الرَّجُلِ يُحْدِثُ يَوْمَ الْعِيدِ، قَالَ: يَطْلُبُ الْمَاءَ، فَلْيَتَوَضَّأْ وَلَا يَتَيَمَّمْ

english.narrator: Mu‘ādh ibn Mu‘ādh → Ash‘ath → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Mu‘ādh ibn Mu‘ādh narrated to us, from Ash‘ath, from Al-Ḥasan, regarding a man who invalidates his wudu on the day of Eid. He said: “He seeks water, performs Wudu, and does not perform Tayammum.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَفَّانُ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا عَطَاءٌ الْخُرَاسَانِيُّ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ، قَالَ: كَانَتِ الصَّلَاةُ الَّتِي أَرَادَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَنْ يُحْرِقَ عَلَى مَنْ تَخَلَّفَ عَنْهَا، صَلَاةَ الْعِشَاءِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → ‘Affan → Hammad ibn Salamah → ‘Ata’ al-Khurasani → Sa‘id ibn al-Musayyib
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Affan narrated to us, saying: Hammad ibn Salamah narrated to us, saying: ‘Ata’ al-Khurasani informed us from Sa‘id ibn al-Musayyib, who said: “The prayer which the Prophet ﷺ intended to burn the houses of those who stayed away from was the ‘Isha prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَفَّانُ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ حُمَيْدٍ، وَغَيْرِهِ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: كَانَتِ الصَّلَاةُ الَّتِي أَرَادَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَنْ يُحْرِقَ عَلَى مَنْ تَخَلَّفَ عَنْهَا، الْجُمُعَةَ

english.narrator: ‘Affan → Hammad ibn Salamah → Humayd → Al-Hasan
english.text: ‘Affan narrated to us, saying: Hammad ibn Salamah narrated to us from Humayd and others, from Al-Hasan, who said: “The prayer which the Prophet ﷺ intended to burn the houses of those who stayed away from was Jumu‘ah.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، فِي حَدِيثٍ ذَكَرَهُ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: هِيَ الْعِشَاءُ أَوِ الْفَجْرُ

english.narrator: Abu Mu‘awiyah → Al-A‘mash → Abu Hurayrah
english.text: Abu Mu‘awiyah narrated to us from Al-A‘mash, from Abu Hurayrah, in a hadith he mentioned from the Prophet ﷺ, that he said: “It is ‘Isha or Fajr.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا الْفَضْلُ بْنُ دُكَيْنٍ، عَنْ زُهَيْرٍ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ أَبِي الْأَحْوَصِ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُهُ مِنْهُ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: هِيَ الْجُمُعَةُ

english.narrator: Al-Fadl ibn Dukayn → Zuhayr → Abu Ishaq → Abu al-Ahwas → ‘Abd Allah
english.text: Al-Fadl ibn Dukayn narrated to us from Zuhayr, from Abu Ishaq, from Abu al-Ahwas—I heard it from him—from ‘Abd Allah, from the Prophet ﷺ, that he said: “It is Jumu‘ah.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سَهْلُ بْنُ يُوسُفَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَوْنٍ، قَالَ: كَتَبْتُ إِلَى نَافِعٍ، أَسْأَلَهُ عَنِ الْقَوْمِ يَكُونُونَ فِي الرُّسْتَاقِ وَيَحْضُرُهُمُ الْعِيدُ، قَالَ: يَجْتَمِعُونَ فَيُصَلِّي بِهِمْ رَجُلٌ وَعَنِ الْجُمُعَةِ، فَكَتَبَ إِلَيَّ: أَمَّا الْعِيدُ فَإِنَّهُمْ يُصَلِّي بِهِمْ رَجُلٌ، وَأَمَّا الْجُمُعَةُ فَلَا عِلْمَ لِي بِهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Sahl ibn Yusuf → Ibn ‘Awn → Nafi‘
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Sahl ibn Yusuf narrated to us from Ibn ‘Awn, who said: I wrote to Nafi‘ asking him about people in the countryside when Eid arrives. He said: “They gather and a man leads them in prayer.” And about Jumu‘ah, he wrote to me: “As for Eid, a man leads them in prayer. As for Jumu‘ah, I have no knowledge about it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، أَنَّهُ قَالَ فِي الْقَوْمِ يَكُونُونَ فِي السَّوَادِ فِي السَّفَرِ فِي يَوْمِ عِيدِ الْفِطْرِ أَوْ أَضْحَى قَالَ: يَجْتَمِعُونَ فَيُصَلُّونَ وَيَؤُمُّهُمْ أَحَدُهُمْ

english.narrator: Ghundar → Shu‘bah → Qatadah → ‘Ikrimah
english.text: Ghundar narrated to us from Shu‘bah, from Qatadah, from ‘Ikrimah, that he said regarding people in the countryside on a journey on the day of Eid al-Fitr or Adha: “They gather and pray, and one of them leads them.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا سَهْلُ بْنُ يُوسُفَ، عَنْ عَمْرٍو، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، فِي أَهْلِ الْقُرَى وَأَهْلِ السَّوَادِ يَحْضُرُهُمُ الْعِيدُ قَالَ: كَانَ لَا يَرَى أَنْ يَخْرُجُوا، فَيُصَلِّي بِهِمْ رَجُلٌ

english.narrator: Sahl ibn Yusuf → ‘Amr → Al-Hasan
english.text: Sahl ibn Yusuf narrated to us from ‘Amr, from Al-Hasan, regarding the people of villages and the countryside when Eid arrives. He said: “He did not see that they should go out [to the city], but rather a man should lead them in prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا الْحَسَنُ بْنُ مُوسَى، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شَيْبَانُ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ أَبِي كَثِيرٍ، قَالَ: سُئِلَ عَطَاءُ بْنُ أَبِي رَبَاحٍ قَالَ: إِذَا كَانَتْ قَرْيَةً جَامِعَةً فَلْيُصَلُّوا رَكْعَتَيْنِ مِثْلَ يَوْمِ الْجُمُعَةِ قَالَ: يَحْيَى: وَسُئِلَ الْحَكَمُ بْنُ عُتَيْبَةَ فَقَالَ: لَا جُمُعَةَ إِلَّا مَعَ الْإِمَامِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ الْجَامِعِ قَالَ: يَحْيَى: وَقَالَ قَتَادَةُ: لَا أَعْلَمُ الْجُمُعَةَ إِلَّا مَعَ السُّلْطَانِ فِي أَمْصَارِ الْمُسْلِمِينَ قَالَ يَحْيَى: "يُقَالُ: لَا جُمُعَةَ، وَلَا أَضْحَى، وَلَا فِطْرَ، إِلَّا لِمَنْ حَضَرَ مَعَ الْإِمَامِ

english.narrator: Al-Hasan ibn Musa → Shayban → Yahya ibn Abi Kathir → ‘Ata’ ibn Abi Rabah/Al-Hakam ibn ‘Utaybah/Qatadah
english.text: Al-Hasan ibn Musa narrated to us, saying: Shayban narrated to us from Yahya ibn Abi Kathir, who said: ‘Ata’ ibn Abi Rabah was asked, and he said: “If it is a village with a congregation, let them pray two rak‘ahs like on Friday.” Yahya said: Al-Hakam ibn ‘Utaybah was asked, and he said: “There is no Jumu‘ah except with the Imam in the congregational mosque.” Yahya said: And Qatadah said: “I do not know Jumu‘ah except with the ruler in the cities of the Muslims.” Yahya said: “It is said: There is no Jumu‘ah, nor Adha, nor Fitr, except for one who attends with the Imam.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَعْمَرُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ الرَّقِّيُّ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، قَالَ: كَتَبَ عُمَرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ إِلَى أَهْلِ الْقُرَى، يَأْمُرهُمْ أَنْ يُصَلُّوا الْفِطْرَ وَالْأَضْحَى، وَأَنْ يُجَمِّعُوا

english.narrator: Ma‘mar ibn Sulayman al-Raqqi → Hajjaj → Al-Hakam → ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Aziz
english.text: Ma‘mar ibn Sulayman al-Raqqi narrated to us from Hajjaj, from Al-Hakam, who said: “‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Aziz wrote to the people of the villages commanding them to pray Fitr and Adha, and to hold Jumu‘ah.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ رَاشِدٍ، عَنْ مَكْحُولٍ، قَالَ: إِذَا كَانَتِ الْقَرْيَةُ لَهَا أَمِيرٌ، فَعَلَيْهِمِ الْجُمُعَةُ

english.narrator: Ishaq ibn Mansur → Muhammad ibn Rashid → Makhul
english.text: Ishaq ibn Mansur narrated to us from Muhammad ibn Rashid, from Makhul, who said: “If the village has an Amir, then Jumu‘ah is obligatory upon them.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي الْعُمَيْسِ، عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْأَقْمَرِ، قَالَ: خَرَجَ مَسْرُوقٌ، وَعُرْوَةُ بْنُ الْمُغِيرَةِ، إِلَى بَدْوٍ لَهُمْ، قَالَ: فَحَضَرَتِ الْجُمُعَةُ، فَلَمْ يُجَمِّعُوا، وَحَضَرَ الْفِطْرُ، فَلَمْ يُفْطِرُوا

english.narrator: Abu Usamah → Abu al-‘Umays → ‘Ali ibn al-Aqmar → Masruq/‘Urwah ibn al-Mughirah
english.text: Abu Usamah narrated to us from Abu al-‘Umays, from ‘Ali ibn al-Aqmar, who said: Masruq and ‘Urwah ibn al-Mughirah went out to a Bedouin settlement of theirs. He said: “Jumu‘ah arrived and they did not hold Jumu‘ah, and Fitr arrived and they did not hold the Fitr prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ حَسَنٍ، عَنْ مُطَرِّفٍ، عَمَّنْ، سَمِعَ عَطَاءً، وَمُجَاهِدًا، قَالَ: يُقْضَى التَّكْبِيرُ فِي الْعِيدَيْنِ كَمَا تُقْضَى الصَّلَاةُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki‘ → Hasan → Mutarrif → Someone who heard ‘Ata’/Mujahid
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki‘ narrated to us from Hasan, from Mutarrif, from someone who heard ‘Ata’ and Mujahid say: “The Takbir in the two Eids is made up just as the prayer is made up.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، قَالَ: يُصَلِّي رَكْعَتَيْنِ، وَيُكَبِّرُ

english.narrator: Yahya ibn Sa‘id → Ibn Jurayj → ‘Ata’
english.text: Yahya ibn Sa‘id narrated to us from Ibn Jurayj, from ‘Ata’, who said: “He prays two rak‘ahs and says the Takbir.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، قَالَ: يُصَلِّي مِثْلَ صَلَاتِهِ، وَيُكَبِّرُ مِثْلَ تَكْبِيرِهِ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Mughirah → Hammad
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us from Mughirah, from Hammad, who said: “He prays like his prayer and says Takbir like his Takbir.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي عَدِيٍّ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَوْنٍ، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ سِيرِينَ، قَالَ: "كَانُوا يَقُولُونَ: إِذَا شَكَّ الرَّجُلُ فِي صَلَاةِ الْمَغْرِبِ فَأَرَادَ أَنْ يُعِيدَ، صَلَّى رَكْعَةً فَشَفَّعَهَا، ثُمَّ صَلَّى ثَلَاثَةً

english.narrator: Muhammad ibn Abi ‘Adi → Ibn ‘Awn → Anas ibn Sirin
english.text: Muhammad ibn Abi ‘Adi narrated to us from Ibn ‘Awn, from Anas ibn Sirin, who said: “They used to say: If a man doubts in the Maghrib prayer and wants to repeat it, he should pray a rak‘ah to make it even, then pray three.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي عَدِيٍّ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَوْنٍ، عَنِ الْقَاسِمِ، مِثْلَهُ

english.narrator: Muhammad ibn Abi ‘Adi → Ibn ‘Awn → Al-Qasim
english.text: Muhammad ibn Abi ‘Adi narrated to us from Ibn ‘Awn, from Al-Qasim, similar to it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ حُصَيْنٍ، عَنْ هِلَالِ بْنِ يَسَافٍ، قَالَ: أَخَذَ بِيَدِي زِيَادُ بْنُ أَبِي الْجَعْدِ وَأَوْقَفَنِي عَلَى شَيْخٍ بِالرَّقَّةِ يُقَالُ لَهُ: وَابِصَةُ بْنُ مَعْبَدٍ، فَقَالَ: صَلَّى رَجُلٌ خَلْفَ الصَّفِّ وَحْدَهُ فَأَمَرَهُ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَنْ يُعِيدَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → ‘Abd Allah ibn Idris → Husayn → Hilal ibn Yasaf → Ziyad ibn Abi al-Ja‘d → Wabisah ibn Ma‘bad
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Abd Allah ibn Idris narrated to us from Husayn, from Hilal ibn Yasaf, who said: Ziyad ibn Abi al-Ja‘d took my hand and stood me before a Shaykh in Al-Raqqah called Wabisah ibn Ma‘bad. He said: “A man prayed alone behind the row, so the Prophet ﷺ ordered him to repeat [the prayer].”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُلَازِمُ بْنُ عَمْرٍو، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ بَدْرٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ شَيْبَانَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ ابْنِ شَيْبَانَ، وَكَانَ مِنَ الْوَفْدِ قَالَ: خَرَجْنَا حَتَّى قَدِمْنَا عَلَى النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، فَبَايَعْنَا وَصَلَّيْنَا خَلْفَهُ، فَرَأَى رَجُلًا يُصَلِّي خَلْفَ الصَّفِّ وَحْدَهُ، فَوَقَفَ عَلَيْهِ نَبِيُّ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ حَتَّى انْصَرَفَ، فَقَالَ: اسْتَقْبِلْ صَلَاتَكَ، فَلَا صَلَاةَ لِلَّذِي خَلْفَ الصَّفِّ

english.narrator: Mulazim ibn ‘Amr → ‘Abd Allah ibn Badr → ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn ‘Ali ibn Shayban → Ibn Shayban
english.text: Mulazim ibn ‘Amr narrated to us from ‘Abd Allah ibn Badr, who said: ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn ‘Ali ibn Shayban narrated to me from his father Ibn Shayban—who was part of the delegation—who said: We went out until we came to the Prophet ﷺ. We pledged allegiance and prayed behind him. He saw a man praying alone behind the row. The Prophet of Allah ﷺ stopped by him until he finished, then said: “Repeat your prayer, for there is no prayer for the one who is behind the row.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مَرْوَانَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: يُعِيدُ

english.narrator: Hafs → ‘Amr ibn Marwan → Ibrahim
english.text: Hafs narrated to us from ‘Amr ibn Marwan, from Ibrahim, who said: “He repeats.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبَّادُ بْنُ الْعَوَّامِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، قَالَ: لَا يَقُمْ وَحْدَهُ

english.narrator: ‘Abbad ibn al-‘Awwam → ‘Abd al-Malik → ‘Ata’
english.text: ‘Abbad ibn al-‘Awwam narrated to us from ‘Abd al-Malik, from ‘Ata’, who said: “He should not stand alone.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنْ جُوَيْبِرٍ، عَنِ الضَّحَّاكِ، عَنْ حُذَيْفَةَ، قَالَ: سُئِلَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ خَلْفَ الصُّفُوفِ وَحْدَهُ، قَالَ: لَا يُعِيدُ

english.narrator: Abu Mu‘awiyah → Juwaybir → Al-Dahhak → Hudhayfah
english.text: Abu Mu‘awiyah narrated to us from Juwaybir, from Al-Dahhak, from Hudhayfah, who was asked about a man alone behind the rows. He said: “He does not repeat.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، وَعَمْرٍو، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: يُجْزِيهِ

english.narrator: Hafs → Ash‘ath/‘Amr → Al-Hasan
english.text: Hafs narrated to us from Ash‘ath and ‘Amr, from Al-Hasan, who said: “It suffices him.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، فِي الرَّجُلِ يَدْخُلُ الْمَسْجِدَ فَلَا يَسْتَطِيعُ أَنْ يَدْخُلَ فِي الصَّفِّ قَالَ: كَانَ يَرَى ذَلِكَ يُجْزِيهِ إِنْ صَلَّى خَلْفَهُ

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-A‘la → Yunus → Al-Hasan
english.text: ‘Abd al-A‘la narrated to us from Yunus, from Al-Hasan, regarding a man who enters the mosque and cannot join the row. He said: “He saw that it suffices him if he prays behind it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُعْتَمِرُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ سَالِمٍ، عَنْ أَبِي الذَّيَّالِ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، فِي الرَّجُلِ يُسْبَقُ بِرَكْعَةٍ، فَيُحْدِثُ الْإِمَامُ، فَيَأْخُذُ بِيَدِ الَّذِي سُبِقَ فَيُقَدِّمُهُ كَيْفَ يَصْنَعُ؟ قَالَ: قَالَ: يُصَلِّي رَكْعَةً وَيَجْلِسُ، ثُمَّ يُثْنِي عَلَى صَلَاةِ الْقَوْمِ، فَإِذَا أَتَمَّ بِهِمْ أَرْبَعًا جَلَسَ فَتَشَهَّدَ، ثُمَّ أَخَذَ بِيَدِ رَجُلٍ فَسَلَّمَ، ثُمَّ قَامَ الرَّجُلُ فَصَلَّى رَكْعَتَهُ الَّتِي سُبِقَ بِهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Mu‘tamir ibn Sulayman → Salim → Abu al-Dhayyal → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Mu‘tamir ibn Sulayman narrated to us from Salim, from Abu al-Dhayyal, from Al-Hasan, regarding a man who misses a rak‘ah, then the Imam invalidates his ablution and pulls forward the one who missed a rak‘ah. How should he act? He said: “He prays a rak‘ah and sits, then builds upon the prayer of the people. When he completes four with them, he sits and recites the Tashahhud, then takes the hand of a man [to lead the Salam] and he says Salam. Then the man stands up and prays the rak‘ah he missed.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَمَّادُ بْنُ زَيْدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الشَّقَرِيِّ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، فِي رَجُلٍ صَلَّى رَكْعَةً فَأَحْدَثَ، فَأَخَذَ بِيَدِ رَجُلٍ فَقَدَّمَهُ وَقَدْ فَاتَتْهُ تِلْكَ الرَّكْعَةُ قَالَ: يُصَلِّي بِهِمْ بَقِيَّةَ صَلَاتِهِمْ، فَإِذَا أَتَمَّ أَخَذَ بِيَدِ رَجُلٍ مِمَّنْ شَهِدَ تِلْكَ الرَّكْعَةَ فَقَدَّمَهُ فَسَلَّمَ بِهِمْ، ثُمَّ قَامَ فَقَضَى تِلْكَ الرَّكْعَةَ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Hammad ibn Zayd → Abu ‘Abd Allah al-Shaqari → Ibrahim
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Hammad ibn Zayd narrated to us from Abu ‘Abd Allah al-Shaqari, from Ibrahim, regarding a man who prayed a rak‘ah then invalidated his ablution, so he took the hand of a man and put him forward, and he had missed that rak‘ah. He said: “He prays with them the rest of their prayer. When he completes it, he takes the hand of a man who witnessed that rak‘ah and puts him forward to say the Taslim with them, then he stands up and makes up that rak‘ah.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ أَرْقَمَ بْنِ شُرَحْبِيلَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لَمَّا جَاءَ إِلَى أَبِي بَكْرٍ وَهُوَ فِي الصَّلَاةِ فِي مَرَضِهِ، أَخَذَ فِي الْقِرَاءَةِ مِنْ حَيْثُ بَلَغَ أَبُو بَكْرٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki‘ → Isma‘il → Abu Ishaq → Arqam ibn Shurahbil → Ibn ‘Abbas
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Isma‘il narrated to us from Abu Ishaq, from Arqam ibn Shurahbil, from Ibn ‘Abbas, that when the Prophet ﷺ came to Abu Bakr while he was in prayer during his illness, he continued the recitation from where Abu Bakr had reached.
